articles.title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
articles.description: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers."

articles.title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
articles.description: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.

articles.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
articles.description: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

articles.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
articles.description: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

articles.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
articles.description: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

articles.title: This is what some of the world’s largest banks of malware look like stacked as hard drives
articles.description: What would some of the world's largest repositories of malware look like if they were stacked as hard drives, one on top of the other?

articles.title: Geothermal startup Fervo Energy pops 33% in IPO debut fueled by AI data center demand
articles.description: Fervo Energy's IPO was upsized several times after potential investors asked why the enhanced geothermal startup wasn't raising more money.

articles.title: X launches a History tab for bookmarks, likes, videos, and articles
articles.description: X’s new History tab combines bookmarks, likes, watched videos, and read articles into a single place, expanding the app’s role as a save-it-for-later tool.

articles.title: Instagram’s new ‘Instants’ feature combines elements from Snapchat and BeReal
articles.description: The feature lets users share disappearing photos with their close friends or mutual followers that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

articles.title: Rivian spinoff Mind Robotics raises another $400M
articles.description: Mind Robotics, which was first revealed in late 2025, has now raised more than $1 billion to date.

articles.title: Who trusts Sam Altman?
articles.description: "I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson," Altman testified in federal court.

articles.title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
articles.description: Origin Lab will serve as a marketplace where AI labs can buy high-quality licensed data, and video-game companies can sell it.

articles.title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners
articles.description: For founders and investors, Anthropic's new offering signals that the AI platform wars are expanding downmarket and that the next major battleground for user acquisition isn't the Fortune 500; it's the 36 million small businesses that make up the backbone of the U.S. economy.

articles.title: Ransomware hackers claim breach at Foxconn, a major electronics manufacturer for Apple, Google, and Nvidia
articles.description: A ransomware group has claimed responsibility for hacking the electronics manufacturing giant Foxconn and is attempting to extort the company.

articles.title: Amazon launches an AI shopping assistant for the search bar, powered by Alexa+
articles.description: Alexa for Shopping offers a voice- and touch-enabled shopping experience across mobile, desktop, and Echo Show smart displays. Alexa for Shopping provides more personalized recommendations and automates the shopping experience across Amazon and other online retailers.

articles.title: Anduril raises $5B, doubles valuation to $61B
articles.description: After achieving $2.2 billion in revenue in 2025, the defense tech startup has raised another massive round, led by Thrive and a16z, it says.

articles.title: r/WallStreetBets really hates the SEC’s proposal to weaken quarterly reporting
articles.description: The retail trading subreddit submitted the sharpest criticism yet of the financial regulator's idea of letting companies report twice per year.

articles.title: US lawmakers demand answers from Instructure after Canvas data breaches
articles.description: U.S. House lawmakers want to know how hackers broke into education tech giant Instructure twice and stole reams of data from students who use the company's flagship student data software Canvas.

articles.title: Anthropic now has more business customers than OpenAI, according to Ramp data
articles.description: A survey compiled from fintech firm Ramp’s clients’ expense data shows 34.4% of participating businesses are paying for Anthropic services, more than any other AI lab, while only 32.3% pay for OpenAI.

articles.title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today’s tougher startup market
articles.description: From October 13-15, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages, led by 250+ tech leaders shaping the industry today. Register now to save up to $410, plus 50% off a second pass.

articles.title: De l'orchestrateur labyrinthe au facilitateur de projet : Retour sur le meetup Airflow 3.2
articles.description: Retour sur le meetup Apache Airflow 3.2 organisé par Astronomer et Ippon Technologies à Paris : Partition Keys pour propager le contexte entre DAGs, Async @task pour paralléliser les micro-tâches, et un REX concret sur l'industrialisation d'Airflow à grande échelle.

articles.title: Mise en forme SEO : comment structurer vos contenus pour maximiser la lecture
articles.description: Un article efficace combine contenu de qualité et mise en forme soignée pour captiver les lecteurs. Les pratiques de présentation, comme l'utilisation de titres clairs, de listes et d'espaces suffisants, améliorent la lisibilité et soutiennent le référencement. Ainsi, une bonne forme maximise…

articles.title: Gemini transforme des notes manuscrites en fiches de révision
articles.description: L'agent conversationnel de Google peut numériser des dizaines de pages, puis les convertir en fiches de révisions ou en flashcards.

articles.title: Google dévoile 5 nouvelles fonctionnalités pour Android : Emoji 3D, Gemini, compatibilité AirDrop…
articles.description: Google a tenu son Android Show : I/O Edition 2026 ce mardi 12 mai. Au programme : Gemini Intelligence, des emojis redessinés en 3D, une dictée vocale améliorée et même un nouveau PC portable.

articles.title: Profils hybrides, power skills, cybersécurité : l’ESGI adapte sa pédagogie aux besoins des entreprises
articles.description: Comment former les experts informatiques à l'ère de l'IA et de la cybersécurité ? Nous avons interrogé Kamal Hennou, directeur de l'ESGI, sur l'adaptation de la pédagogie aux nouveaux enjeux du secteur.

articles.title: Quelles études et formations suivre pour devenir community manager ?
articles.description: Diplôme académique ou certification courte, les voies pour exercer le métier de community manager sont nombreuses. Tour d'horizon des formations à privilégier selon son profil et ses objectifs.

articles.title: Social media : quels KPI faut-il vraiment suivre en 2026 ?
articles.description: Followers, likes, taux d'engagement... Les professionnels du social media ont accès à des dizaines de métriques. Mais lesquelles méritent vraiment d'être suivies ? Quatre experts partagent leur approche.

articles.title: #356.src - La force de la conférence: Pourquoi les devs ont besoin de se retrouver IRL avec Julien Landuré
articles.description: "Notre métier est profondément ancré dans une culture de transmission, de personne à personne." Le D.E.V. de la semaine est Julien Landuré, CTO chez TechTown. Organisateur engagé de conférences comme le DevFest Nantes, il nous explique pourquoi la culture tech adore se retrouver en chair et en os,…

articles.title: Medicare’s new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
articles.description: There is no governmental mechanism to pay for an AI agent that monitors a patient between visits, calls to check in, coordinates a housing referral, or makes sure someone picks up their medication. ACCESS creates that mechanism for the first time.

articles.title: CrowdSec : la défense réseau communautaire expliquée

articles.title: Kevin Hartz’s A* just closed its third fund with $450M
articles.description: The firm takes a generalist approach, backing companies across categories such as AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security. The average check size for this fund will be between $3 million and $5 million, with the aim to back at least 30 startups.

articles.title: Former Tesla exec and Heron Power CEO Drew Baglino has founded a heat pump startup
articles.description: Sadi Thermal Machines is Drew Baglino's second startup since leaving Tesla in 2024.

articles.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
articles.description: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his experience running the prominent startup accelerator Y Combinator, knew "founders who had control usually…

articles.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
articles.description: "Any sale or transfer of Anthropic stock, or any interest in Anthropic stock, offered by these firms is void and will not be recognized on our books and records," the company's support page reads.

articles.title: Potholes cost cities millions: This company is using AI and trucks to fix them
articles.description: Fleet management company Samsara has developed an AI model to detect different kinds of potholes and gauge how fast they're deteriorating.

articles.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit
articles.description: Google and SpaceX are in talks to build data centers in orbit, pitching space as the future home for AI compute, even as costs today remain far higher than on the ground.

articles.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets
articles.description: Google unveiled its new AI-first Googlebooks laptops, more agentic Gemini features, vibe-coded Android widgets, Gemini in Chrome, refreshed Android Auto, and more ahead of I/O.

articles.title: The AI legal services industry is heating up — Anthropic is getting in on the action
articles.description: Anthropic's new tools are designed to help law firms automate specific clerical functions — things like document search and review, case law resources, deposition prep, document drafting, and other related areas.

articles.title: Google adds Gemini-powered dictation to Gboard, which could be bad news for dictation startups
articles.description: Google's transcription feature will initially launch with Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel phones.

articles.title: Android adds a feature to stop you from doomscrolling
articles.description: Google’s new Android feature, Pause Point, forces you to wait before opening distracting apps in an effort to curb addictive scrolling habits.

articles.title: Google unveils Googlebook, a new line of AI-native laptops
articles.description: The company says Googlebooks, which are launching this fall, are the first laptops designed from the ground up for Gemini Intelligence to offer personal and proactive help.

articles.title: Google’s ‘Create My Widget’ feature will let you vibe-code your own widgets
articles.description: To create a widget, users will be able to describe what they want using natural language. For example, you could ask the feature to "suggest three high-protein meal prep recipes every week" in order to get a custom dashboard that you can add and resize on your home screen.

articles.title: Google brings agentic AI and vibe-coded widgets to Android
articles.description: Gemini Intelligence will also include Gboard-based dictation and form-filling capabilities.

articles.title: Google launches new Android security feature to help uncover spyware attacks
articles.description: Intrusion Logging is a new part of Android’s Advanced Protection Mode, which aims to help protect human rights activists, journalists, and dissidents from government spyware attack and law enforcement forensic devices.

articles.title: Indigo brings the open social web to one app
articles.description: Indigo's new social app lets you cross-post to the open social web, including Mastodon and Bluesky, and browse a unified timeline.

articles.title: US bank discloses security lapse after sharing customer data with AI app
articles.description: Community Bank, which operates in Pennsylvania, Ohio, and West Virginia, disclosed a cybersecurity incident that exposed customers’ names, dates of birth, and Social Security numbers.

articles.title: Threads tests a Meta AI integration that works similarly to Grok
articles.description: The feature is designed to help people get real-time context about trends and breaking stories, as well as receive recommendations, all within conversations.

articles.title: Exaforce raises $125M Series B to build AI for catching and stopping cyberattacks as they happen
articles.description: As bad actors weaponize AI to exploit software vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed, companies are increasingly recognizing the need to bolster their cybersecurity defenses. The round valued the three-year-old startup at $725 million.

articles.title: Waymo issues recall to deal with a flooding problem
articles.description: Waymo has issued a software recall that makes its robotaxis more cautious around flooded areas. A "final remedy" is in the works.

articles.title: WhatsApp Plus : l’abonnement payant de Meta arrive sur iPhone
articles.description: Après plusieurs semaines de tests, WhatsApp Plus est disponible sur iOS pour un nombre limité d'utilisateurs. L'offre, qui est facturée 2,49 € par mois, mise principalement sur des options de personnalisation.

articles.description: La robotique de service est en train de changer d’échelle. articles.description: Fondateur de Seiven, Alexis Jubert a formalisé une charte IA au sein de sa PME. Il nous partage sa démarche, des premiers usages non encadrés aux lignes rouges qu'il a posées pour protéger ses collaborateurs.

articles.title: Thinking Machines wants to build an AI that actually listens while it talks
articles.description: Right now, every AI model you've ever used works the same way. You talk, it listens. It responds, you listen. Thinking Machines is trying to change that by building a model that processes your input and generates a response at the same time, so it's more like a phone call than a text chain.

articles.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
articles.description: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

articles.description: Five-year old European military drone startup Helsing is close to raising another monster round.

articles.description: As microdrama apps like ReelShort and DramaBox quietly rake in billions, Peacock announced on Monday that it is launching two unscripted Bravo microdramas, which will stream in the Peacock app.

articles.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
articles.description: In an email to beta testers, the company said the site's goal is to "track the most influential voices in a space" and to surface the news that's actually worth "paying attention to."

articles.title: ‘Too early’ to talk IPO, Redwood Materials’ incoming CFO says
articles.description: The battery-recycling and energy-storage company has hired former Tesla finance chief Deepak Ahuja, reuniting him with former Tesla CTO JB Straubel.

articles.title: NYT’s Wordle to become a TV game show
articles.description: This will be the first time that The Times has collaborated with a TV broadcaster for an entertainment-based program, representing yet another pivot in the media company's attempt to build a sustainable digital subscription business as print revenue continues to decline.

articles.title: TikTok launches an ad-free subscription plan in the UK
articles.description: Users who sign up for the plan won’t see ads on TikTok, and their data won’t be used for advertising purposes.

articles.title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
articles.description: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

articles.title: Discord launches Nitro Rewards, giving subscribers access to the base tier of Xbox Game Pass for no extra cost
articles.description: Discord's Nitro Rewards program will give Nitro subscribers access to Xbox Game Pass and discounts from Logitech, SteelSeries, and other gaming brands.

articles.description: TeePee est une solution SaaS no-code développée à Lille, permettant aux entreprises de digitaliser efficacement leurs processus métier sans complexité technique. articles.title: Venmo’s biggest makeover in years comes at a very interesting time
articles.description: The timing is notable. PayPal, which owns Venmo, is restructuring to spin Venmo off as a stand-alone business unit — a move widely seen as laying the groundwork for a potential sale. Stripe has reportedly expressed interest in buying PayPal outright.

articles.title: Korea’s biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
articles.description: Instead of building robots themselves, the team is focused on a simpler goal — providing data that robots need to learn and operate. They believe that better data will be key to making robots more useful.

articles.title: Étude : l’IA, une béquille pour les jeunes souffrant d’anxiété
articles.description: Une enquête Ipsos bva publiée en mai 2026 dresse un état des lieux des usages de l'IA chez les 11-25 ans. Les résultats révèlent des usages qui dépassent largement le scolaire et un recours croissant à l'IA pour des questions intimes.

articles.title: Les 20 langages informatiques les plus populaires en mai 2026
articles.description: Python conserve la tête du classement, tandis que Swift, R, Rust et Perl signent les meilleures progressions du mois.

articles.title: Étude LinkedIn : qu’est-ce qui marche encore en 2026 ?
articles.description: Une étude de Social Insider analyse 1,3 million de posts LinkedIn et révèle que l'engagement global augmente, surtout via les documents natifs. Pourtant, les vues de vidéos chutent de 36 %, et la croissance des abonnés ralentit, car les utilisateurs se tournent vers d'autres plateformes pour…

articles.title: Quelle personnalité de la tech se cache derrière cette citation ou anecdote ?
articles.description: Depuis plusieurs décennies, ils ont façonné les appareils, le web et les usages. Mais parviendrez-vous à les identifier grâce à des anecdotes ou des petites phrases ?

articles.description: Days after the disclosure of a critical vulnerability in popular web hosting software cPanel and WHM, hackers are now targeting and hacking thousands of vulnerable websites.

articles.title: Elon Musk’s only AI expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race
articles.description: Stuart Russell is a long-time AI researcher who thinks governments need to restrain frontier labs.

articles.title: Sierra raises $950M as the race to own enterprise AI gets serious
articles.description: The raise gives Sierra more than $1 billion to work with — capital the company says it will use to become the "global standard" for AI-powered customer experiences.

articles.title: Elon Musk sent ominous texts to Greg Brockman, Sam Altman after asking for a settlement, OpenAI claims
articles.description: Musk texted OpenAI's president and co-founder saying that he and CEO Sam Altman "will be the most hated men in America" if OpenAI doesn't settle the suit.

articles.title: Meet the Finalists: JetBrains x Codex Hackathon
articles.description: Put a capable coding model inside a developer’s primary workspace, and the IDE stops being a place where you write code. articles.title: Anthropic and OpenAI are both launching joint ventures for enterprise AI services
articles.description: Both Anthropic and OpenAI have partnered with asset managers to more aggressively market their enterprise AI products.

articles.title: As X shuts down Communities, Acorn debuts an alternative that puts creators in control
articles.description: Acorn lets organizations build and run their own online communities using decentralized tech, with custom feeds, moderation, and analytics tools.

articles.title: US healthcare marketplaces shared citizenship and race data with ad tech giants
articles.description: Virginia and Washington, D.C. paused the data collection and sharing, after Bloomberg's investigation found their health insurance marketplaces were sharing users' information with advertisers.

articles.title: Amazon opens up its global logistics network to all businesses
articles.description: The new service, called Amazon Supply Chain Services, pits the e-commerce giant directly against UPS and FedEx.

articles.title: 5 days only: Bring a partner or colleague and get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
articles.description: The BOGO offer is live. For a limited time, buy one pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get 50% off a second of the same ticket type. Offer ends this Friday, May 8. Save here.

articles.title: We Gave Agents IDE-Native Search Tools. They Got Faster and Cheaper.
articles.description: We ran the same coding tasks with and without prebundled tooling, across multiple models and languages. Here’s what changed. articles.title: Barocal can cool your food and drink by squeezing a hunk of plastic crystals
articles.description: Barocal might have discovered a cheap, nonpolluting material that could dethrone today's refrigeration technology.

articles.title: DoorDash adds AI tools to speed up merchant onboarding, edit photos of dishes
articles.description: DoorDash on Monday added new AI-powered tools that let merchants speed up onboarding, edit photos to make dishes look better, and create new websites from existing content.

articles.title: Novabricks : la plateforme No-Code « à la française » pour réconcilier les métiers et la DSI
articles.description: Novabricks est une plateforme "aPaaS" qui facilite la création d'applications métiers sans codage grâce au No-Code et Low-Code. Elle répond aux besoins numériques croissants des entreprises, aidant à surmonter les limitations des solutions traditionnelles. Novabricks permet ainsi une rapidité,…

articles.title: Symfony AI Mate : un troisième port d'entrée pour votre app Symfony
articles.description: Découvrez Symfony AI Mate, la solution innovante qui ouvre votre application Symfony aux agents via JSON-RPC sur stdio. Exposez vos services à des IDE ou chatbots en toute sécurité, sans port TCP ouvert. Simplifiez le développement avec cette approche unique.
articles.source: Le Code est dans le Pré

articles.title: Ouster’s new color lidar is coming to replace cameras
articles.description: A sensor that can simultaneously capture depth and image data has long been a "holy grail," Ouster CEO Angus Pacala told TechCrunch.

articles.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber, le nouveau modèle d’OpenAI qui rivalise avec Mythos
articles.description: Capable de détecter des vulnérabilités, d'analyser des malwares ou d'effectuer des pentests, GPT-5.5-Cyber s'annonce comme le pendant d'OpenAI à Mythos. Son déploiement restera, lui aussi, très encadré.

articles.title: Claude : 10 astuces pour économiser ses tokens
articles.description: Ces derniers mois, de nombreux utilisateurs se retrouvent bloqués avant la fin de leur session Claude, sans vraiment comprendre pourquoi. Voici quelques astuces pour optimiser vos tokens !

articles.title: Comment sécuriser votre compte ChatGPT
articles.description: OpenAI dévoile Advanced Account Security, un ensemble de protections renforcées pour les comptes ChatGPT, avec un partenariat inédit avec Yubico pour l'authentification par clé physique.

articles.title: Nicolas Sauvage is betting on the boring parts of AI
articles.description: The portfolio he has assembled since 2019 is dotted with technologies that have become more widely interesting to VCs over the last year.

articles.title: 5 formations en ligne pour créer et piloter une boutique en ligne
articles.description: Notre sélection de formations de la semaine est dédiée à celles et ceux qui souhaitent se lancer dans l'e-commerce, en créant leur boutique en ligne et en développant leurs ventes.

articles.title: Deux ans après son lancement, Le Chat rivalise-t-il avec ChatGPT ?
articles.description: Lancé en février 2024, Le Chat a suscité un certain enthousiasme en France, s'imposant comme une alternative crédible aux solutions américaines. Mais a-t-il, deux ans après, rattrapé une partie de son retard sur ChatGPT ? BDM a testé.

articles.description: Within hours he'd thrown up a website — a janky, one-hour job, by his own admission — and by Sunday, 36,000 "founding patrons" had pledged nearly $23 million, crashing his servers in the process.

articles.title: ‘This is fine’ creator says AI startup stole his art

articles.title: In Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors
articles.description: A new study examines how large language models perform in a variety of medical contexts, including real emergency room cases — where at least one model seemed to be more accurate than human doctors.

articles.title: Musely secures $360M from General Catalyst without giving up equity
articles.description: The DTC skin, hair, and menopause care brand will use the non-dilutive capital to super-charge customer acquisition.

articles.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions

articles.title: Ubuntu services hit by outages after DDoS attack
